ai_boundary.reason: The locally measured multibillion-model latency is seconds, while this screen measures subsecond repricing. AI may later supply an asynchronous market-level veto only after matched after-cost uplift evidence.

economic_contract.active_tick_contract: Create each order with the latest locally known same-segment tick, then reject the simulated fill if that exact limit is invalid under the first post-target execution-confirmation book's active tick. Tick changes are never backfilled from initial market metadata.
economic_contract.entry: Buy one outcome with a FOK order against the first same-segment book locally observed after one per-leg submission latency plus the recorded venue taker delay; require that confirmation within 500 ms and walk all displayed asks up to the valid market price ceiling.
economic_contract.execution_confirmation_semantics: A post-target book is a deliberately conservative execution-confirmation state, not a claim that it was the exact hidden exchange state at arrival. Its receipt time starts the holding clock, matching the shared replay and paper-execution convention.
economic_contract.exit: Sell the exact bot-owned quantity with a FOK order against the first same-segment book locally observed after the confirmed entry, fixed holding interval, second per-leg submission latency, and recorded venue taker delay; require confirmation within 500 ms and walk displayed bids down to the valid market price floor.
economic_contract.fee_source: Use each market's recorded fee schedule independently at every walked price level on both legs.
economic_contract.fee_precision: Round each simulated displayed-level fee upward to five USDC decimals. This is conservative because official documentation specifies five-decimal precision but does not specify tie direction or hidden maker-order decomposition.
economic_contract.legacy_fee_rate_endpoint: Record and hash both token fee-rate payloads, but do not add their V1 base_fee value to V2 feeSchedule charges. Polymarket's March 31, 2026 changelog designates the market feeSchedule as the V2 calculation source.
economic_contract.minimum_order_quantity: The recorded market minimum order size; no subminimum notional is invented.
economic_contract.order_construction: Use the official V2 share-sized limit-order builder and post the signed order as FOK. Do not reinterpret BUY quantity through the quote-amount createMarketOrder convenience API.
economic_contract.prepositioned_inventory_upper_bound: The exit assumes equivalent bot-owned outcome inventory is already available, so onchain buy-confirmation delay cannot block the sale. This is deliberately optimistic and cannot be reported as a realizable strategy return.

execution_parameter_contract.base_fees: Record and hash maker_base_fee and taker_base_fee but do not add them to V2 feeSchedule charges. The official V2 client omits fee_rate_bps from V2 signed orders, and the March 31, 2026 changelog designates feeSchedule as the calculation source.
execution_parameter_contract.evidence_required: Every replay market must have integrity-verified CLOB market snapshots observed no later than the simulated server arrival. Missing parameters reject that decision.
execution_parameter_contract.minimum_order_age_seconds: Recorded and hash-bound for audit, but not reinterpreted as taker latency because official documentation defines it separately. A nonzero value rejects the decision until exact FOK semantics are documented and modeled.
execution_parameter_contract.taker_delay_semantics: When the latest recorded itode flag at simulated server arrival is true, add the officially documented 250 ms taker delay independently to each marketable leg; otherwise add zero. Unknown never means zero.

hypothesis.alternative: At least one fixed latency/holding cell has broad, depth-executable, positive after-fee market-level opportunity support across BTC, ETH, and SOL.
hypothesis.null: Observable short-horizon Polymarket repricing is too small or too fast to survive two taker spreads, two fee charges, displayed-depth capacity, and realistic latency.
hypothesis.purpose: Reject an economically impossible fast-taker family before fitting ML or invoking AI. Future books are used only for an explicitly noncausal opportunity ceiling.

promotion_gate.positive_margin: Best complete round-trip net proceeds for a market must exceed zero after exact depth and both fees; one best opportunity per market and outcome is retained.
promotion_gate.required_outcome_breadth: Each asset must contain at least five positive Up and five positive Down market opportunities in the primary cell.

risk_boundary.final_30_seconds_excluded: Both entry and exit venue-arrival targets must remain at least 30 seconds before market end; filtering only the initial decision is insufficient.
risk_boundary.reason: Settlement-window flow, binary payoff convexity, and insufficient reaction time make the final interval a distinct manipulation and close-risk regime; this first mechanism screen does not optimize against it.
risk_boundary.unknown_or_missing_book_action: Exclude the opportunity and record the reason; never synthesize a fill or carry a book across a gap or connection segment. A bounded post-target book may confirm execution but is never described as the exact exchange state at arrival.

source_contract.development_use_of_segmented_capture: Permitted only as a mechanism diagnostic with confirmation_eligible=false.
source_contract.future_book_use: Only the oracle ceiling and realized execution label may read future books. No future state is a model feature.
source_contract.receive_clock_contract: Use local monotonic arrival for causal ordering, elapsed latency, holding duration, freshness, and post-target bounds. Persist local wall and monotonic receive values plus all derived targets in each signed opportunity. Wall time anchors UTC market start/end and is not allowed to reorder events because operating-system wall-clock corrections can move independently of monotonic time.
